parts.text: Perform a code review of `git diff HEAD` for current working changes. Return a markdown list of only actionable bug findings (critical/high). For each finding include a specific fix prompt with file and line range (format `@path:line`). If no critical findings, return `- No critical issues found.`

state.title: Shows working changes against HEAD

/Users/jaronswab/go/src/github.com/jrswab/axe/docs/plans/025_url_fetch_spec.md:
  Line 1: # Specification: `url_fetch` Built-in Tool
  Line 13: Implement the `url_fetch` tool — a built-in tool registered in the `Registry` that performs an HTTP GET request and returns the response body as text. This tool is needed for feature parity when axe replaces the custom container worker in Agent Engine.
  Line 63: **Requirement 1.1:** Add a new constant `URLFetch = "url_fetch"` to the `const` block in `internal/toolname/toolname.go`.
  Line 67: ### 3.2 `url_fetch` Tool Definition
  Line 69: **Requirement 2.1:** Create a file `internal/tool/url_fetch.go`.
  Line 74: - `Name`: the value of `toolname.URLFetch` (i.e., `"url_fetch"`)
  Line 86: ### 3.3 `url_fetch` Tool Executor
  Line 126: **Requirement 5.3:** The `toolname_test.go` test `TestConstants_Values` currently has 6 entries (including `CallAgent`). After adding `URLFetch`, this test must be updated to include a case for `{"URLFetch", URLFetch, "url_fetch"}`.
  Line 130: **Requirement 6.1:** Update the `Scaffold()` function in `internal/agent/agent.go` to include `url_fetch` in the commented tools list. The comment should read:
  Line 132: # Valid: list_directory, read_file, write_file, edit_file, run_command, url_fetch
  Line 148: │       ├── url_fetch.go             # NEW: Definition, Execute, helper entry func
  Line 149: │       ├── url_fetch_test.go        # NEW: tests
  Line 151: │       ├── registry_test.go         # MODIFIED: add url_fetch registration tests
  Line 284: ### 7.2 `internal/tool/url_fetch_test.go` Tests
  Line 322: **Update: `TestConstants_Values`** — Add a table entry: `{"URLFetch", URLFetch, "url_fetch"}`.
  Line 364: | `internal/tool/url_fetch.go` exists and compiles | `go build ./internal/tool/` succeeds |
  Line 365: | `toolname.URLFetch` constant exists with value `"url_fetch"` | `TestConstants_Values` passes |
  Line 366: | `ValidNames()` includes `url_fetch` | `TestValidNames_ReturnsExpectedCount` (6) and `TestValidNames_ContainsAllExpectedNames` pass |
  Line 367: | `RegisterAll` registers `url_fetch` | `TestRegisterAll_RegistersURLFetch` passes |
  Line 385: | Registry registers url_fetch | `TestRegisterAll_RegistersURLFetch` passes |
  Line 386: | Registry resolves url_fetch with correct params | `TestRegisterAll_ResolvesURLFetch` passes |
  Line 387: | Scaffold comment lists url_fetch | Manual verification of `Scaffold()` output |
  Line 437: - **No path validation needed.** Unlike file tools, `url_fetch` operates on URLs, not file paths. The scheme validation (`http`/`https` only) is the security boundary, preventing `file://` access to the local filesystem.
  Line 442: - **No `Workdir` usage.** Unlike file tools, `url_fetch` does not use `ExecContext.Workdir`. The tool operates on network URLs, not filesystem paths. `Workdir` is irrelevant.
  Line 443: - **The `RegisterAll` pattern continues.** This adds the sixth tool: `list_directory`, `read_file`, `write_file`, `edit_file`, `run_command`, `url_fetch`.
  Line 444: - **Security model.** The `url_fetch` tool allows the LLM to make arbitrary HTTP GET requests to any `http://` or `https://` URL. This is by design — if an agent has `tools = ["url_fetch"]` in its TOML config, the user has explicitly opted in. Scheme validation prevents `file://` abuse. Network-level restrictions (firewalls, egress policies) are outside axe's scope.

<content>1: package toolname
2: 
3: // Tool name constants for agent configuration.
4: // These are the canonical string values used in TOML config files
5: // and tool dispatch logic.
6: const (
7: 	CallAgent     = "call_agent"
8: 	ListDirectory = "list_directory"
9: 	ReadFile      = "read_file"
10: 	WriteFile     = "write_file"
11: 	EditFile      = "edit_file"
12: 	RunCommand    = "run_command"
13: 	URLFetch      = "url_fetch"
14: )
15: 
16: // ValidNames returns the set of tool names that can appear in an agent's
17: // tools configuration field. Each call returns a new map instance.
18: // CallAgent is excluded because it is controlled by the sub_agents field.
19: func ValidNames() map[string]bool {
20: 	return map[string]bool{
21: 		ListDirectory: true,
22: 		ReadFile:      true,
23: 		WriteFile:     true,
24: 		EditFile:      true,
25: 		RunCommand:    true,
26: 		URLFetch:      true,
27: 	}
28: }

(End of file - total 28 lines)
